config_files.notes: Primary settings file. Internal game codename is 'Madness' — do NOT look for an 'Outriders' folder. Section header is [/Script/Madness.MadGameUserSettings]. Several settings (CameraFov, FrameRateLimit, bFrameRateLimitEnabled, bMotionBlurEnabled, DLSSQuality) are stored inside a single compound value called DisplaySettings on one line, not as standalone keys.
Used for low-level engine overrides such as r.Streaming.PoolSize. On a fresh install the file contains only [Core.System] content paths. Add a [/Script/Engine.Engine] section for any engine CVars.

keys.effect: Horizontal field of view in degrees. Range: 75–130°. Game is confirmed Hor+ — FOV widens automatically at ultra-wide aspect ratios, but the baseline value still needs to be set high enough.
keys.notes: This key lives INSIDE the DisplaySettings compound value, not as a standalone line. Default of 75 is the slider minimum and causes tunnel-vision on all aspect ratios. On 32:9 displays (e.g. 5120×1440) push to 100–110° to counteract the extreme peripheral motion that causes motion sickness. On 16:9 displays, 85–95° is the comfortable range. Edit the compound value carefully — the entire DisplaySettings= line must remain on one line.

keys.effect: Enables or disables the in-game frame rate limiter. Must be True for FrameRateLimit (inside DisplaySettings) to take effect.
keys.notes: Defaults to False (no cap). Always enable alongside FrameRateLimit to prevent uncapped frame times. Lives inside the DisplaySettings compound value.

keys.effect: Frame rate cap inside the Outriders DisplaySettings compound value. Known bug: after certain in-game menu interactions this value can be serialised as a corrupt integer (e.g. 1131675648). Set to a valid float and ensure bFrameRateLimitEnabled=True.
keys.notes: This is a DUPLICATE of the standard UE4 FrameRateLimit standalone key. BOTH must be set to avoid confusion. The corrupt-integer bug only affects this compound copy; the standalone key (same file, outside DisplaySettings) holds a normal float. Lives inside the DisplaySettings compound value.

keys.effect: Standard UE4 frame rate cap (standalone key, not inside DisplaySettings). Set this in tandem with the compound copy above.
keys.notes: Appears as a standalone key towards the end of the [/Script/Madness.MadGameUserSettings] section. Default is 0.000000 (uncapped). Must be set in sync with the FrameRateLimit inside DisplaySettings.

keys.effect: Controls how much the cinematic camera lags behind input ('floaty' camera smoothing). 1.0 = maximum smoothing; 0.0 = immediate camera response with no lag.
keys.notes: Default of 1.0 introduces input-to-camera desynchronisation that is a well-documented motion sickness trigger, especially during fast movement and ability use. Recommended Off (0.0) for all goals.

keys.effect: Frame rate cap applied during in-engine cutscenes. Separate from the gameplay FrameRateLimit.
keys.notes: Default is 60. Mismatching this with gameplay FrameRateLimit causes a jarring pacing drop on cutscene transitions. Set to match your gameplay cap. Cutscene FPS options per PCGamingWiki: 30, 60, 90, 120.

keys.effect: Enables or disables motion blur. Lives inside the DisplaySettings compound value.
keys.notes: Defaults to False in most installs. Recommended Off for all goals. Lives inside the DisplaySettings compound value.

keys.effect: NVIDIA DLSS upscaling quality preset. Known values: 0=Off, 1=Performance, 2=Quality. Higher values produce sharper images at greater GPU cost.
keys.notes: Only available on NVIDIA RTX GPUs. At 5120×1440 (32:9) DLSS Quality (2) is recommended even for a quality goal — the resolution is demanding enough that DLSS Quality renders at ~72% scale with near-native sharpness. Lives inside the DisplaySettings compound value.

keys.effect: Texture streaming pool size in MB. Higher values reduce texture pop-in and blurry surface streaming on GPUs with sufficient VRAM.
keys.notes: Not present in Engine.ini by default. Add the [/Script/Engine.Engine] section header before this key if the file only contains [Core.System] paths. Scale with VRAM: ~1000 for 4–6 GB, ~2000 for 8 GB, ~4000 for 12 GB+. RTX 4070 SUPER (12 GB) warrants 4000.

engine_overrides.behaviour: Outriders duplicates this key: once as a standalone UE4 key (FrameRateLimit=0.000000) and once embedded inside the game-specific DisplaySettings compound value. The compound copy can be serialised as a corrupt integer (e.g. 1131675648) after certain in-game menu interactions. The standalone key remains a valid float.
engine_overrides.action: Set both copies to the same value. Fix the compound copy by replacing the integer with a valid float (e.g. 120.000000) and set bFrameRateLimitEnabled=True inside the same compound.

engine_overrides.behaviour: Many per-game keys (CameraFov, bFrameRateLimitEnabled, FrameRateLimit, bMotionBlurEnabled, DLSSQuality, bUseVSync, bUseHDRDisplayOutput, ResolutionScaling) are stored as sub-fields of a single compound value on one line, not as individual INI keys. Standard UE4 key-lookup tooling will not find them.
engine_overrides.action: Edit the entire DisplaySettings= line. Ensure the line stays on a single line with no newlines inserted. The section is [/Script/Madness.MadGameUserSettings].

manual_only_settings.reason: Mouse smoothing is enabled by default. It introduces input-to-cursor lag that compounds the camera smoothing issue. Disabling it makes aiming and camera control immediately responsive, reducing motion sickness during fast gameplay.

manual_only_settings.location: Settings → Accessibility or Settings → Gameplay (if present)
manual_only_settings.reason: Screen shake during combat and ability use is a motion sickness trigger. Not exposed as a config key. Check Accessibility settings — availability may vary by game version.

intro_skip.label: Rename splash video file
intro_skip.notes: The Steam install folder is typically named 'Outriders'. The internal subdirectory 'Madness' matches the game's codename. Renaming rather than deleting allows reverting by restoring the original filename. If a game update recreates the file, repeat this step.

intro_skip.label: Make startup movies skippable via Game.ini
intro_skip.notes: Verified working. Overrides the game's shipped defaults (bMoviesAreSkippable=False, bWaitForMoviesToComplete=True). An ESC-to-skip prompt appears at the lower-right of each startup movie; pressing Escape dismisses it immediately. The videos still play until skipped — !StartupMovies=ClearArray has no effect in this game. Must be in Game.ini, NOT Engine.ini (Outriders ignores this section in Engine.ini). Can be combined with the file-rename entry above to both prevent playback and retain the ESC fallback for any movies a game update might restore.

notes: Internal game codename is 'Madness'. Config files are at %LOCALAPPDATA%\Madness\, NOT %LOCALAPPDATA%\Outriders\.
Game requires a constant internet connection for all modes (always-online DRM).
Easy Anti-Cheat was removed in the Worldslayer update (28 July 2022).
Cutscene FPS is governed by CineMaxFramerate separately from gameplay FrameRateLimit. Default cutscene cap is 60 FPS — set to match gameplay cap to avoid pacing jolt on transitions.
WMI (Win32_VideoController) misreports VRAM for GPUs with >4 GB due to a 32-bit integer overflow. Use GPU-Z or NVIDIA/AMD control panel for accurate VRAM when sizing r.Streaming.PoolSize.
